title: Adopt chezmoi as the dotfile manager
outcome: chezmoi source spec drafted under packages/dust/dotfiles/ (draft, dry-run only): .chezmoi.toml.tmpl declares the `profile` data var; dot_zshrc.tmpl sources the guarded Dust shell fragment; dot_gitconfig.tmpl holds non-secret git config. No live $HOME or ~/.local/share/chezmoi writes.
validation: validate.sh PASS — structure + naming, template balance, no-secrets all pass; chezmoi-render check DEFERRED (chezmoi binary not installed; E03 owns install)
decisions: chezmoi source lives as a draft spec under packages/dust/dotfiles/ (dust owns shell substrate + lists chezmoi in its `dotfiles` module); promotion to ~/.local/share/chezmoi/ is human-gated
the epic's `.chezmoi.profile` is implemented as a custom `profile` data var (chezmoi has no built-in .profile) set in .chezmoi.toml.tmpl from $WFOS_PROFILE
dot_zshrc.tmpl does not hardcode DUST_HOME; it sources dust.zsh only when DUST_HOME is set (avoids the Tech/Dev vs Build path discrepancy in the existing dust.zsh — left for E04 namespace alignment)
open_observations: packages/dust/config/shell/dust.zsh default DUST_HOME points at the old Tech/Dev/... path, not Build/...; out of scope for E01, flag for E04 namespace alignment
token_note: loaded 1 story block + 1 canon ref + protocol/state/schema vs the 14-doc roadmap + ~20-doc canon; validator output is compact PASS/FAIL, not raw chezmoi logs
